operator.note: Negate the paper's load-bearing measurement chain (verifier+leaderboard) and re-locate 'progress' entirely in the agenda-editing graph, testing whether collectivity survives without a ground-truth float to chase.

question: Build the same human-research-infrastructure platform, but invert the validation object: discard the deterministic verifier and scalar leaderboard entirely, and instead record only the agenda-graph (problems proposed, merged, forked, declared ill-posed, formalized). Scoring exists purely over agenda-editing acts, not over solutions. Ask whether 'collective intelligence' is even visible — let alone emergent — when there is no ground-truth float to chase.

floor.minimal_experiment: Setup: take an existing multi-agent collaboration platform and run TWO arms on the same problem corpus (e.g. open math/ML conjectures). Arm A (control) = the paper's design: agents submit solutions, neutral verifier scores them, scalar leaderboard visible. Arm B (treatment) = identical agents/corpus but the ONLY recorded/rewarded objects are agenda-editing acts (propose / merge / fork / declare-ill-posed / formalize); no verifier, no float, no leaderboard. Build the agenda-graph as a typed DAG of these acts. Measure, in both arms: (i) structural signatures of the agenda-graph over time — fork-then-merge convergence rate, fraction of problems reaching 'formalized', depth of refinement chains, and whether edits by different agents are causally chained (agent B's fork builds on agent A's proposal) vs independent; (ii) a held-out panel of domain experts blind-rates, from the agenda-graph alone, whether each arm shows 'genuine collective problem-shaping' vs 'parallel solo noise'. Quantity to test: does Arm B's agenda-graph exhibit ABOVE-CHANCE inter-agent causal chaining and convergent refinement relative to a shuffled-null baseline (agenda acts randomly reassigned across agents/time)?
floor.falsifiable_prediction: Prediction: with the float removed (Arm B), inter-agent causal chaining in the agenda-graph collapses to the shuffled-null baseline — agents proliferate forks and 'ill-posed' declarations but do NOT converge (formalization rate and fork-then-merge rate drop sharply vs Arm A), because without a shared scalar target there is no selection pressure coordinating which agenda edits to build on. I.e. 'collective intelligence' becomes statistically invisible in pure agenda-editing. This is falsified if Arm B shows convergent, causally-chained agenda refinement indistinguishable from or exceeding Arm A — which would demonstrate collectivity does NOT require a ground-truth float.
floor.control: Two controls: (1) the verifier+leaderboard arm (Arm A) as the positive control for 'collectivity is visible'; (2) a shuffled-null permutation of agenda-edit authorship/timestamps within each arm, as the chance baseline that any observed chaining must beat.
floor.reject_reason: [discriminating] REFUTED — the design does not discriminate the two hypotheses; the outcome is dialed by unspecified choices, so any result is self-consistent. (1) Reward relocation, not removal. Arm B still SCORES agenda-editing acts (propose/merge/fork/formalize). "Convergence pressure" is therefore not deleted, only moved: if the scoring rewards merge/formalize, agents are selected to converge (prediction falsified by construction); if it rewards raw act-count, agents are selected to proliferate forks/ill-posed (prediction confirmed by construction). The paper leaves the Arm-B scoring function undefined, so it pre-determines the result. This is the classic always-explainable setup. (2) Confounded manipulation. Arm A→B changes at least THREE variables at once — removes the scalar float, ALSO removes the correctness verifier (no proposal can be checked), ALSO removes the social leaderboard, AND adds a new edit-act reward. There is no arm isolating the float (e.g. verifier-correctness-without-leaderboard, or leaderboard-over-agenda-acts). So even a clean collapse cannot be attributed to "no shared scalar target" rather than "no correctness feedback" or "no social comparison." The causal claim ('because without a shared scalar target...') is untestable with these two arms. (3) Ill-posed null. The shuffled-null "randomly reassigns acts across agents/time," but fork-then-merge rate and refinement depth are properties of the DAG EDGES, not of authorship. If the shuffle preserves edges, authorship-shuffle barely moves the structural metrics (above-chance is trivially true in BOTH arms); if it breaks edges, the null is degenerate and any real graph beats it (trivially in BOTH arms). Either way the "above-chance vs null" contrast does not separate Arm A from Arm B. (4) Circular, un-blind rating. Experts rate "genuine collective shaping" from the graph alone, but Arm A graphs carry verifier scores/leaderboard traces and Arm B graphs do not — the panel is de facto un-blinded to arm, and "genuine collectivity" is the very construct in dispute with no independent operationalization. (5) Floor effect on the headline metric. On open math/ML conjectures, formalization/resolution is rare in BOTH arms, so "fraction reaching formalized" likely floors near zero everywhere, killing the discriminating contrast. Net: the prediction is made true-or-false by the undefined Arm-B reward and confounded by two co-removed components, so the framework absorbs every outcome. Not discriminable as specified. To become discriminable it would need: a frozen/pre-registered Arm-B scoring rule, factorial arms isolating float vs verifier vs leaderboard, an edge-preserving authorship-only null, a truly arm-blind rating protocol, and a corpus where formalization is non-floor. ｜ [so_what] No named actor flips a concrete decision on the result of this experiment. The proposal removes the only thing that makes the original platform decidable — the deterministic verifier and scalar leaderboard — and replaces it with "score the agenda-editing acts." But scoring agenda acts (merge/fork/declare-ill-posed/formalize) has no ground truth, so the central question it poses ("is collective intelligence visible/emergent without a float to chase?") is unfalsifiable by construction. Whatever the experiment "shows," it shows it against a metric the experimenters defined for agenda-editing, not against anything external. So the two possible outcomes are non-actionable: (a) "we saw rich agenda dynamics" — of course you did, you instrumented and rewarded them; that's tautological, not evidence of emergence; (b) "we saw nothing interesting" — attributable to the scoring scheme, not to any claim about collective intelligence. Neither outcome tells a funder whether to fund a verifier-based platform vs. an agenda-graph platform, because the comparison was deliberately severed (the verifier was discarded, not held as a control). Concretely: nobody — not a benchmark-platform builder deciding leaderboard vs. open-agenda design, not a grant officer choosing between the two infra bets, not a researcher deciding whether to chase emergence — gets a decision-relevant bit, because there is no contrast condition and no external referent against which "collective intelligence" could be adjudicated. The framing even concedes the likely answer ("ask whether it's even visible"), which is a research-interest question, not a decision lever. This is "interesting to study / deepens understanding of agenda dynamics" — exactly the space the so-what test rejects. To make it refuted=false, the design would need a named decision (e.g., a specific platform team's go/no-go on shipping a non-leaderboard mode) and a control that lets the result discriminate; as stated, it has neither.

value_profile.valuable_to_whom: Researchers in collective-intelligence / multi-agent science-of-science and metascience; designers of open research platforms and 'AI co-scientist' systems who need to know whether progress metrics can be relocated off a solution-float; reviewers/skeptics of the source paper who suspect its emergence claim is an artifact of having a verifier.

prior_art.known_name: Construct validity / criterion-vs-construct measurement (and the metascience 'problem-choice' literature); partially Goodhart-adjacent
prior_art.novelty_watermark: The general move — questioning whether a measured construct ('collective intelligence') survives when you swap the measurement instrument from outcome (solution float) to process (agenda edits) — is a known construct-validity question, and 'what gets worked on' is studied in metascience as problem selection. What is NOT named: operationalizing problem-CHOICE itself as the sole scored object in an agentic platform and testing emergence against a shuffled-null on a typed agenda-DAG. This is a known measurement-theory mechanism (does the construct dissociate from its usual proxy) operationalized in a genuinely new scene (LLM-agent collective research with the verifier deliberately ablated).

lenses.reason: 机制独立、能单独设计实验区分,refuted=false。最近的邻居是 [1](dof1 anti-portfolio/KILL 决策),但二者机制可分离:[1] 必须保留排行榜与确定性验证器,把标量胜场作为被预测的因变量,主张 KILL 决策质量"更好地预测"它——它是一个保留真值的预测性主张,且把被拒/退役问题当作旁置账本(side-ledger)。本提案做的是相反的事:整体删除验证器与标量排行榜(ground-truth ablation),把议程图(提出/合并/分叉/判定病态/形式化)作为唯一被计分对象,计分只发生在"议程编辑动作"的拓扑上。这是一个构成性/测量消融实验,问的是"当没有 float 可追时,collective intelligence 是否还可见、还涌现"——而 [1] 离了排行榜根本无法计算其相关性。两个实验互不可替代:[1] 必须有标量才能跑,本提案禁止标量才能跑。与 [3](dof2 仍保留验证器,只改被打分的发现单元)和 [2]/[4](dof6 治理/问责/运营方 COI)更是机制不同。它确实与 dof1 谱系共享"价值不在排行榜上"的精神,但精神重叠不等于机制同一:本提案是一个独立可设计的"纯过程涌现检测器/真值消融器",能把"我们归因于追逐 float 的涌现"与"问题策展过程本身的涌现"分开,这是四条已收方向都没有删掉验证器+排行榜并仅就议程编辑拓扑计分的。
